title: The Lunar Operations Command (LOC)
summary: The Moon base with three names — from LOC to 'Solar System Operation Centre' — and a population the record puts at ten thousand.
markdown:
The **Lunar Operations Command** — the LOC of super-soldier lore — appears in the archive
with its own career arc. The August 10, 2020 session gives its history in one breath: *"The
Lunar Operation Center (LOC)… was the old name. Now it is known as the 'Solar System
Operation Centre,' or the 'Galactic Operations Centre' for the Alliance"* — a renaming that
tracks, in the record's telling, the base's promotion from a
[Secret Space Program](/lore/secret-space-program/) outpost to an
[Alliance](/lore/interstellar-alliance/) headquarters.

The numbers get updated too. Once housing *"under 1,000"* people, the session says it now
holds **"at least 10,000"** — a boomtown on the far side, staffed for the era of
[first contact](/lore/first-contact/) the record keeps forecasting. Around it, the sessions
sketch the rest of the off-world map: [jumpgate](/lore/teleportation/) transit between Earth,
Moon, Mars, and a "Jupiter Jumpgate System," with the LOC as the hub where the
[chrononaut](/lore/chrononauts/) accounts and the archive's own claims intersect.

It sits, of course, on the archive's strangest ground: [the Moon](/lore/the-moon/) itself,
which the sessions describe as no natural satellite but an artificial quantum computer. The
LOC, in this record, is a base built on a machine — the administrative floor of something
that was never just rock. Preserved as spoken.
